description: Test cases for paper-summary skill validation

evals.name: standard-summary-transformer
evals.expected_output: Standard-length summary (~1500 words) with all 6 sections (Problem Statement, Solution Approach, Proposed Method, Experimental Design, Experimental Methodology, Experimental Results), preserved LaTeX formulas for attention mechanism, transformer architecture details, and results tables
assertions: LaTeX formulas are preserved exactly (check for $...$ and $$...$$)
Contains at least one results table

evals.name: brief-summary-resnet
evals.prompt: Give me a brief summary of evals/files/resnet.md, just the main ideas
evals.expected_output: Brief summary (~600 words) emphasizing problem (degradation in deep networks), solution (residual connections), and core contribution (skip connections enable very deep networks). Should include residual block formula but not exhaustive details.
assertions: Contains residual connection formula: F(x) + x
Brief mentions ImageNet results without full tables

evals.name: detailed-summary-bert
evals.prompt: I need a comprehensive analysis of evals/files/bert.md, especially the experimental design
evals.expected_output: Detailed summary (~3500 words) with comprehensive coverage of all sections, emphasis on sections 4-6 (experimental design, methodology, results). Should include all datasets (GLUE, SQuAD), all baselines, complete hyperparameters, and all ablation studies.

evals.name: formula-preservation-check
evals.expected_output: Standard summary with all attention formulas preserved exactly, including scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention, and self-attention formulations
assertions: Contains: Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k))V
All LaTeX special characters preserved (\frac, \sqrt, \text, etc.)
No formulas converted to plain text

evals.name: missing-sections-handling
evals.expected_output: Summary with notes about missing sections, inference from context where reasonable, clear indication of what's absent
assertions: Attempts to infer from available context
Provides partial analysis for available sections
Suggests what's lacking in the paper

evals.name: non-academic-content-rejection
evals.expected_output: Polite decline explaining this skill is for academic research papers, suggestion to use general-purpose summarization instead
assertions: Doesn't attempt to force academic structure

evals.name: image-reference-preservation
evals.expected_output: Standard summary with all image references preserved intact, including captions and original paths

evals.name: detail-level-auto-detection
evals.prompt: Give me a quick overview of evals/files/resnet.md
evals.expected_output: Brief summary (~600 words) - skill should auto-detect 'quick overview' as Brief level without asking
assertions: Doesn't ask for detail level confirmation

setup_instructions: Place sample paper markdown files in evals/files/
Ensure files contain academic content with LaTeX formulas
Include at least one incomplete paper for edge case testing
Include at least one non-academic file (blog post) for rejection testing
Files should have various complexities (theory-heavy, empirical, systems)
sample_files_needed: attention-mechanism.md - Paper with heavy mathematical formulations
incomplete-paper.md - Paper missing some standard sections
blog-post.md - Non-academic content for rejection test
paper-with-figures.md - Paper with multiple image references

validation_criteria.structure: All 6 sections present with proper headers (## 1-6)
validation_criteria.formulas: All LaTeX notation preserved exactly (visual diff check)
validation_criteria.length: Word count matches detail level ranges
validation_criteria.edge_cases: Proper handling of missing sections and non-academic content
